violations.text: No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Employees were observed using phone then going directly back to the food without washing their hands or changing gloves. Food employees must wash their hands before putting on gloves. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking proper signage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2026

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. Employee said there was no replacement for the empty liquid quaternary ammonia container, so there was no sanitizer. Service area used tablets, which were then used in the 3 bay sink. Bleach was observed stored under the sink, and also in the basement storage area, but no one present knew it was a sanitizer. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Chicken salad tortilla wraps packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels, with sub-ingredients missing for Willow Tree chicken salad, and the tortillas. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Wraps were removed from the self service case and placed in the display case. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
Water observed in several stacked food containers on the clean storage rack. After cleaning and sanitizing, equipment and utensils must be air dried. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the basement storage area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2026
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2026
The non-food contact surfaces of the chest freezer in the basement have an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2026

The floors in the basement storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The insulation attached to the Max Fan above the flattop grill is in poor repair, with dirty edges hanging down. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2026

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the kitchen. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2026

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The door to the outside, located in the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The concentration of the sanitizer solution for the wet wiping cloths was >400 parts per million (ppm). The required concentration for quat sanitizer is 200 ppm. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025
Inspector observed employee drying dishes with a cloth from the dishwasher. Dishes must be allowed to air dry. New Violation. Correct By: 02/12/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. PIC signed up for a food safety class. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Various items in the 2 door reach in were observed held between 50-59 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded items. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Yogurt & granola parfaits and bowls packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels (missing full list of ingredients and business address). Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name and address. PIC moved the items behind the counter. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025

The door to the outside, located at the entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 02/11/2025
Wet wiping cloths are not stored in a chemical sanitizer between uses. Wet wiping cloths must be stored in a chemical sanitizer at the appropriate concentration. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The two door reach-in cooler located in the kitchen area was not in good repair (58 degrees). Refrigeration units must hold food at or below 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Not to be used to hold TSC foods until unit is fixed. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025
Dishwasher was observed in poor repair. Dishwasher was not able to get up to proper sanitizing temperatures. Dishwasher taken out of service. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The wall in the basement area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. Management's plan for assessing, monitoring, and controlling foodborne disease hazards is inadequate. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. English Responding to Vomiting and Diarrhea Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Staff was instructed how to properly set up 3 bay sink and test sanitizer. 3 bay sink will be used to sanitize until dishwasher is functioning properly. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Egg whites were observed held at 73 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC discarded. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Milk was observed held at 44-47 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Milk was placed in freezer to rapidly chill. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Soup was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Yogurt parfaits and bowls, soups, and overnight oats packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 01/24/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the basement storage area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Knives observed stored in crack between inside wall and bracket of prep unit, and in the crack behind a shelf rail in the storage area between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the basement storage area.Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
Dishwasher was observed in poor repair. Dishwasher was not able to get up to proper sanitizing temperatures. Dishwasher taken out of service. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2025

Cardboard is used to line food storage shelves in the kitchen and basement areas.Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary ammonia sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 01/15/2025
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 01/15/2025
The receptacle in the toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored under the counter on top of business items. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the shelf beneath the counter have an accumulation of dirt and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 10/23/2023
No person in charge was present as needed at the time of inspection. Owner arrived during inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Cut lemons observed in same container as whole unwashed tomatoes and radishes. Disposed. Food must be protected from contamination by packaging, covered containers, or wrapping. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Prosciutto, and milk observed held at 47-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Prosciutto was disposed of, and milk was placed in refrigerator. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

The establishment is lacking a thermometer to test food temperatures. Food temperature measuring devices shall be provided and be readily accessible for checking cooking, cooling, reheating, and hot/cold holding temperatures of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. New Violation. Correct By: 10/13/2023

Working containers of nuts and matcha poweder are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Fruit parfaits, peanut butter balls, and Brekkie bowls packaged in the food establishment are lacking complete labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Items were removed from the self service case and placed into display case by PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023
Food observed stored on the floor near the deli display cooler, and beneath wire shelves. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Staff observed wearing several pieces of dangling jewelry. Except for a plain ring such as a wedding band, while preparing FOOD, FOOD EMPLOYEES may not wear jewelry including medical information jewelry on their arms and hands. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Knives stored on an unclean surface (beneath the panini press) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Linens observed stored on the floor beneath hanging brooms at the top of the basement stairs. Linens must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

The ice bucket was observed stored on the floor beneath the hand sink in the kitchen. Equipment and utensils must be stored in a clean dry location, where they are not subject to dust, splash, or other contamination, and at least 6 inches above the floor. Ice bucket was taken to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Single service items are not being stored properly in their original protective package and/or are stored on a surface that is not clean in the cooking and coffee areas. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Plastic wrap (out of box) observed stored on the floor near the prep unit.Single service items observed stored on the floor in the basement.Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Plastic wrap observed lining the top of the under-counter refrigerator. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment that are exposed to splash, spillage, or other food soiling or that require frequent cleaning must be constructed of a nonabsorbent and smooth material. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the milk refrigerator, and display case (including door tracks) have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored beneath the cash register counter. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/12/2023

A wall in the ice machine area of the basement is in poor repair, with evidence of water damage and peeling paint. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/05/2023
